topic: Someone discovers their teenager has a secret social media account
raw_output:
[story 1]

The phone screen glowed with an image that made my jaw tighten. I had been cleaning out Leo’s old tablet, a task I’d been putting off for weeks, and this was the treasure trove. Tucked deep within a rarely accessed folder, I found it: a login screen for an account I didn't recognize. My blood pressure spiked. I clicked. The feed was a curated highlight reel of filtered selfies and cryptic, vaguely provocative captions. A cold, hard knot formed in my stomach. I slammed the tablet onto the desk, the sound echoing sharply in the quiet room. "Leo!" I shouted, my voice tight with something sharp and brittle. He walked in, whistling, and froze instantly, his eyes wide with a sudden, panicked awareness. The silence stretched, thick and suffocating, punctuated only by my ragged breathing.

[story 2]

I found the password easily enough, a combination of his birth year and a random number he’d used in middle school. The account unlocked, and the sheer volume of carefully constructed digital personas hit me like a physical blow. It wasn't just a secret; it was a whole parallel life I hadn't known existed. My hands started shaking as I scrolled through posts that felt alien, manufactured. Every perfectly angled picture, every carefully chosen filter, screamed of a disconnect I hadn't anticipated. I stood up abruptly, pacing the length of the living room, my knuckles white where I gripped the edge of the sofa. When my son finally appeared in the doorway, looking bewildered, I didn't bother with pleasantries. "What is this?" The question was low, edged with a dangerous flatness that made him shrink back.

[story 3]

It was the sheer audacity of it that truly set me off. I’d stumbled upon the evidence—a hidden profile, a carefully maintained digital shadow—and the feeling that I had been completely bypassed, completely fooled, churned into a furious heat inside me. I stared at the screen, my breath catching in short, sharp gasps. This wasn't just teenage rebellion; this felt like a deliberate deception. I snatched the device, my movements jerky and fast, and marched toward Leo’s room. I didn't knock. I threw the door open, the sound of splintering wood almost lost beneath the sudden, intense tension radiating off me. "Stop," I commanded, my tone dangerously level, every syllable weighed down with displeasure. He flinched, dropping the game controller he was holding, the sound of plastic hitting the carpet sounding deafeningly loud in the sudden confrontation.
